title: Aluminum price drops as Trump moves to narrow levies on metal products
summary: Aluminum prices fell 0.7% to settle at $3,077.50/ton on the LME as traders reacted to reports that President Trump may roll back some import tariffs on aluminum and steel products. The White House has communicated to companies that adjustments to levies on derivative metal products are in the works, though Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent downplayed any potential rollback.

title: Aluminum prices surge to a four-year high amid war
summary: Aluminum prices reached their highest levels in nearly four years, driven by multiple market pressures including tariffs, supply constraints, and geopolitical tensions. The surge reflects broader base metal strength and continued bullish sentiment in the commodities market.

title: Scrap Metal Market Recap | Aluminum 4¢ Copper 2¢
summary: Aluminum led the scrap metal market with EC wire rising 4 cents per pound, while copper moved higher by 2 cents on the ScrapMonster Price Index. Brass, stainless, and steel prices remained unchanged during the period.

title: Daily Update — March 11, 2026
summary: The war in the Middle East has disrupted aluminum production and exports in Gulf Cooperation Council countries by stalling maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, a key corridor for raw materials and products. This has driven up aluminum prices and raised supply shortage concerns, worsened by the closure of South32's Mozal smelter in Mozambique.

title: Mercuria to withdraw nearly 100,000t of aluminum from LME
summary: Commodity trader Mercuria plans to withdraw nearly 100,000 tons of aluminum from LME warehouses in Port Klang, Malaysia, amid the Strait of Hormuz shutdown freezing Middle East shipments. Middle East producers like Alba and Qatalum face production reductions and force majeure, straining supplies in Europe and the US where physical premiums have soared.

title: High Midwest Premium, ongoing tariffs, Iran conflict hit aluminum
summary: Aluminum input costs for metal packaging remain pressured in 2026 due to high Midwest Premiums exceeding $1 per pound, ongoing Section 232 tariffs, and the new Middle East conflict involving Iran. Can manufacturers report passing costs to consumers but expect further price increases, with reliance on imports complicating supply.

title: Aluminium extends gains as supply concerns amid Mideast conflict linger
summary: Aluminum prices surged on Thursday due to persistent global supply tightening from the ongoing Middle East conflict. Disruptions continue to fuel concerns over production capacity and availability.

title: Green grant awarded: Advancing circular aluminium for sustainable water treatment
summary: Alumichem has received a green grant to develop a circular innovation project that replaces virgin aluminium with recycled aluminium in aluminium chlorohydrate (ACH) production for drinking water and wastewater purification. The project, supported by Denmark's Environmental Technology Development and Demonstration Programme (MUDP) and conducted in collaboration with Stena Recycling and Stena Aluminium, aims to significantly reduce energy use, CO₂ emissions, and material waste while maintaining high quality standards for drinking water applications.

title: Novelis Wins Award for 100% Recycled End-Of-Life Vehicle Scrap Product
summary: Novelis Inc. received the Münchner Management Kolloquium (MMK) Award of Excellence 2026 for developing an aluminium sheet made from 100% end-of-life vehicle scrap for exterior car body applications. The award recognizes the company's innovation in sustainable automotive material development and demonstrates the technical feasibility of car-to-car recycling for high-quality aluminium applications, representing a significant step toward decarbonizing the automotive sector.

title: New steel and aluminum tariff rates take effect March 12, executive orders show
summary: New 25% tariff rates on steel and aluminum imports into the United States are set to take effect on March 12, according to executive orders signed by President Donald Trump on Monday. A US official had previously indicated the rates would start on March 4, but the full texts of the orders were released hours after signing. These tariffs are expected to impact steel market dynamics significantly.

title: New steel and aluminum tariff rates take effect March 12, executive orders show
summary: The new 25% tariffs on US steel and aluminum imports will take effect on March 12, as per executive orders signed by President Donald Trump. An earlier announcement suggested March 4, but the orders' full texts were delayed in release. This development is likely to influence steel pricing and trade flows.

title: Tariffs, demand boost US steel outlook: Nucor
summary: Nucor reports historic order backlogs entering 2026, up nearly 40% annually in steel mills and 15% in products, supported by tariffs and demand. The company highlights ongoing issues like transshipping via Mexico and Canada under USMCA renegotiations and limited improvement in automotive and construction sectors. Overall demand is expected to rise slightly in 2026.

title: Major steel and raw material futures prices in China - March 10, 2026
summary: SteelOrbis provides closing prices for major steel futures in China on March 10, 2026, including rebar at RMB 3,104/mt ($450/mt). Recent updates note rising longs prices in Turkey and Saudi Arabia amid demand, with weekly changes showing gains in rebar and HRC. The Orbis Steel Index indicates positive trends for Turkish rebar.

title: Trump to Roll Back Some Tariffs on Steel, Aluminum
summary: US President Donald Trump plans to scale back some steel and aluminum tariffs, causing aluminum prices to drop to a one-week low on the London Metal Exchange and Shanghai Futures Exchange. Trading has slowed in China ahead of the Lunar New Year break. Other metals like copper also saw declines amid broader market movements.

title: Domestic prices for construction steel products have risen by 2.5-2.7% since the beginning of the year
summary: In Ukraine, prices for construction rolled steel products remained stable in February 2026 but have risen 2.5-2.7% since the start of the year. This trend reflects ongoing market adjustments in the region. No specific daily price changes were detailed for the past 24 hours.

title: Agricultural Waste Fuels Green Steel Breakthrough for Australia-India Partnership
summary: Researchers have successfully used agricultural waste to produce biogas for iron ore reduction in India, advancing green steel production. This innovation supports sustainable steelmaking by leveraging renewable biomass sources. The Australia-India partnership highlights potential for scalable low-carbon steel technologies.

title: Copper futures prices reach highest levels of 2025. 3/12/25
source: YouTube (The 11th Hour With Stephanie Ruhle, MSNBC)
summary: Copper futures rose after Chinese smelters cut output and planned maintenance, amid investor monitoring of potential continued reductions. This movement occurs alongside stabilizing gold futures above $2,900 an ounce due to tariff plans and recession fears. The report highlights technical indicators suggesting possible higher prices despite some weakness signals.

title: Copper Futures Prices Rise Across 2026 Contracts on January 12
summary: Copper futures traded higher, with the most actively traded March 2026 contract closing at $5.9535 per pound, up $0.0510 from the previous settlement. Trading volume was 41,846 contracts, with total session volume at 48,303, lower than the prior day. The report provides analysis on US copper demand, supply, trade flows, and forecasts to 2035.

title: Viewpoint: US copper faces uncertain 2026 outlook
summary: Goldman Sachs forecasts copper prices averaging $5.17/lb in 2026, up from $4.82/lb in 2025, despite a small market surplus and no shortage until 2029. Other analysts like Citigroup predict prices above $5.90/lb by Q2 2026, while Bank of America and TD Cowen forecast $5.13/lb and $5.25/lb respectively. The copper market saw a narrowing surplus in 2025, with US CME warehouse stocks reaching record highs.

title: Copper price nears $12,000 as base metals stage broad rally
summary: Copper settled 0.9% higher at $11,881.50 a ton amid a broad base metals rally, with aluminum up 1% at $2,945 a ton. Nickel advanced on prospects of reduced supply from Indonesia, which plans to cut nickel ore output to 250 million tons in 2026 from 379 million this year due to price slumps. Analysts suggest nickel may outperform other base metals entering 2026 if cuts proceed.

title: Why Record-High Copper Prices Aren’t Forecast to Last
summary: Goldman Sachs raised its copper price forecast for the first half of 2026 but expects declines later following US refined copper tariff clarity, projected for mid-2026 announcement and 2027 implementation. Prices above $13,000/t are not expected to sustain, with a fair fundamental value around $11,500/t and Q4 2026 forecast at $11,200/t. Recent rises stem from LME warehouse withdrawals, AI-related demand, and tariff uncertainty boosting US stockpiles.

title: Copper theft ticks up as prices surge
summary: Copper theft is increasing in South Florida amid surging copper prices. The report describes an 'evolution' in theft patterns linked to market trends. This highlights secondary market impacts of rising prices.

title: Copper Tumbles 12% from Record High as Goldman Warns 'Don't Chase the Rally'
summary: Copper prices declined over 3% to settle at $12,740 per tonne, erasing 12% from a record high of $13,387 reached two weeks prior. Global copper smelting activity fell to its lowest level in January in nearly a decade, with 14.3% of global smelting capacity inactive. Processing fees have fallen into negative territory, causing smelters outside China to reduce production.

title: Viewpoint: US copper faces uncertain 2026 outlook
summary: US copper prices are expected to remain volatile in 2026 due to Chinese demand fluctuations, supply constraints, and AI-driven infrastructure needs. Market participants forecast an approaching supply deficit based on lack of new mine commissions and declining South American output, though the copper market saw a 94,000-tonne surplus in the first three quarters of 2025.

title: Why copper's pause may be more about policy than demand
summary: Copper prices have cooled after rallying above $13,000 per tonne, with trade policy, US tariff risks, and shifting Chinese demand emerging as key factors shaping market direction. The consolidation phase reflects complex forces beyond electrification demand, including AI-driven energy infrastructure pressures.

title: Copper in the Age of AI
summary: A comprehensive study projects global copper demand to rise 50% to 42 million metric tons by 2040 from 28 million in 2025, driven by its essential role in electricity generation, transmission, and AI technologies. However, significant supply obstacles could lead to a 10 million metric ton shortfall without major expansions. This underscores copper's pivotal position in technological innovation and the energy transition.

title: Copper Wins Contract for Battery-Equipped Induction Stoves
summary: Clean energy company Copper secured a contract with New York authorities as part of a $32 million investment to develop and produce 10,000 battery-equipped induction stoves for public housing. This initiative supports sustainable domestic supply chains for critical battery materials. It highlights innovations in energy-efficient cooking technology aligned with sustainability goals.

title: Buenaventura Trapiche Copper Project Gets Environmental Approval
summary: Buenaventura's Trapiche copper project in Peru's Apurimac region has received environmental approval, unlocking a $3.4 billion development. This advancement supports expanded copper production essential for technological and sustainable energy applications. The project represents a key step in addressing growing global copper demand.

title: LME nickel on Mar 10 gains slightly as geopolitical concerns ease, China economic indicators improve
summary: The London Metal Exchange (LME) nickel futures price on March 10 slightly increased by US$19/ton to US$17,488/ton, with spot prices also edging up by US$14/ton. The modest gains were driven by easing geopolitical concerns and improving Chinese economic indicators.

title: Nickel Hits 19-month High
summary: Nickel increased to 18,908.00 USD/T, the highest since June 2024. Over the past 4 weeks, nickel gained 19.58%, and in the last 12 months, it increased 21.08%.

title: Nickel Prices Hit $18,000 in 2026 Amid Global Oversupply, US Boosts Domestic Supply Chain
summary: Global nickel prices edged up 0.51% to $17,599.25 per ton, driven by supply-side constraints in Indonesia following strict ore production quotas and operational halts. Resilient demand from Chinese stainless steel mills and EV battery producers provides structural support, though analysts expect prices to settle between $15,000-$16,000 per tonne.

title: Indonesia orders world's biggest nickel mine to cut ore quota for 2026
summary: Indonesia has ordered PT Weda Bay Nickel, the world's largest nickel mine, to reduce its 2026 ore production and sales quota to 12 million wet metric tonnes from 42 million in 2025. The company plans to apply for an increase, while this fits into a broader national cut in nickel ore approvals to 260-270 million tonnes for 2026. Nickel prices rose 2.2% to $17,880/t on the London Metal Exchange following the news.

title: Indonesia Shakes Global Nickel Market: World's Largest Mine Sees 70% Production Cut in Push to Inflate Prices
summary: The Indonesian government has slashed PT Weda Bay Nickel's 2026 production quota to 12 million wet metric tonnes, a 71% reduction from 2025, impacting global nickel supply. Nickel prices surged over 2% to nearly $18,000 per tonne amid shifting market narratives from surplus to shortage. Analysts anticipate potential ore shortages, possibly leading to imports from the Philippines if the quota holds.

title: Indonesia to cut nickel mining quota in 2026
summary: Indonesia's energy ministry will reduce the 2026 nickel production quota to 260-270 million tonnes, down from 379 million tonnes in 2025 and below estimated consumption of 330 million tonnes. This cut aligns with market expectations but may lead to smelter production reductions or increased imports. The decision contributes to ongoing supply tightening in the nickel market.

title: Nickel Industries showcases sustainability in quarterly update
summary: Nickel Industries reported achieving zero lost-time injuries over 12 months with 17.7 million safe man-hours and a Total Recordable Injury Frequency Rate of 0.68. The company completed a strategic acquisition of a 10% stake in Excelsior Nickel Cobalt and received national recognition through the Indonesia Leadership Award 2025 and CNBC Indonesia's Excellence in Sustainability Leadership Award.

title: Nickel Prices Hit $18,000 in 2026 Amid Global Oversupply, US Boosts Domestic Supply Chain
summary: Nickel prices face volatility in 2026 amid global oversupply and rising EV demand. Battery-grade nickel demand is expected to triple by 2030, driven by mid- and high-performance electric vehicles. The US is advancing the Nikolai Project in Alaska, recognized as the largest documented nickel resource in the United States, to strengthen domestic supply chains.
